misconception: High acidity in a wine means the wine is low quality or unpleasant.
why_incorrect: Acidity is a structural component, not a quality indicator in isolation. High acidity in a cool-climate Riesling or Champagne is not a defect — it provides freshness, food-pairing versatility, and the backbone for long-term ageing. Whether acidity is pleasant depends on its balance with fruit intensity, residual sugar, and other wine components. An unbalanced high-acid wine may be unpleasant, but the acidity itself is not the cause of poor quality.
corrected_understanding: High acidity can be a hallmark of quality in cool-climate wines. Its contribution to wine quality depends on whether it is balanced by other components — particularly fruit concentration, sweetness, and alcohol.
detection_signals: This wine is too acidic so it must be poor quality
So high acidity means the wine is lower quality
Doesn't high acidity mean the wine is bad
If it's too acidic it must be poor quality
This wine is too sharp so it probably got a low score
High acidity detracts from the quality of the wine

coaching_content.confusion_statement: You seem to be confusing: High acidity in a wine means the wine is low quality or unpleasant.
coaching_content.evidence_statement: This is based on your answers showing this pattern.
coaching_content.improvement_signal: Correctly explaining the causal relationship in similar questions
